cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to find the sum of all integer bases $ b > 9 $ for which $ 17_b $ divides $ 97_b $. Hmm, let me start by understanding what these numbers mean in base $ b $. First, $ 17_b $ in base $ b $ translates to $ 1 \times b + 7 $ in decimal. Similarly, $ 97_b $ would be $ 9 \times b + 7 $ in decimal. The problem states that $ 17_b $ is a divisor of $ 97_b $, so in decimal terms, this means that $ (1b + 7) $ divides $ (9b + 7) $. So, translating the divisibility condition: $ (b + 7) $ divides $ (9b + 7) $. Therefore, there exists some integer $ k $ such that $ 9b + 7 = k(b + 7) $. Alternatively, another way to express divisibility is that $ (9b + 7) $ divided by $ (b + 7) $ should result in an integer. Let me write that as a fraction: $ \frac{9b + 7}{b + 7} $. To simplify this fraction, maybe I can perform polynomial division or manipulate it algebraically. Let me try to rewrite the numerator
